title: campanula cochleariifolia subsp. jaubertiana
intro: Campanula cochleariifolia subsp. jaubertiana, also known as the bellflower, is a plant species within the Campanula genus. This plant is commonly used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Campanula cochleariifolia subsp. jaubertiana include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, and then infusing them in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
